title: CSE 102 — Minimum Weight Spanning Tree (UCSC, Fall 2021)
description: The programming-project assignment for CSE 102 (Algorithm Analysis & Design): given a weighted undirected graph, compute its minimum weight spanning tree using Prim's algorithm with a priority-queue. The C++ implementation from the original assignment runs unchanged — graph.cpp + graph.h, ~250 lines — compiled to WebAssembly via Emscripten. The JavaScript layer takes input in the same line-format the CLI used, calls into wasm, and renders the resulting MST as a circular-layout graph with red edges marking the spanning tree.

assets.title: MWST — Prim's Algorithm
assets.description: Paste a weighted undirected graph (line format: vertex count, edge count, then one edge per line as 'a b weight') or pick a preset; the demo computes the MST and visualizes it. The C++ Prim's implementation is the original course submission, unmodified. Total weight matches the exact output the CLI produces — verified byte-for-byte against test/in{1,2,3} fixtures.
